rendered:
Rohan, you've completed 4 weeks on the foundation split with excellent adherence and strength gains. We're now progressing to a Upper/Lower/Upper/Lower/Full/Full split to increase frequency on compound movements and allow for better recovery distribution. Your main lifts are up 2.5kg, so we're adding 15% volume while maintaining your 90-minute session duration. Continue hitting squats and rows as primary movements.

Day 1 Upper A: Barbell Bench Press 4x8, Barbell Rows 4x8, Incline Dumbbell Press 3x10, Cable Rows 3x10, Lateral Raise 3x12, Tricep Pushdown 3x12.

Day 2 Lower A: Barbell Squat 4x8, Romanian Deadlift 3x10, Leg Curl 3x12, Leg Press 3x10, Calf Raise 3x15.

Day 3 Upper B: Dumbbell Bench Press 4x8, Seal Rows 4x8, Barbell Rows 3x10, Dumbbell Flyes 3x12, Face Pulls 3x12, Barbell Curl 3x10.

Day 4 Lower B: Barbell Squat 4x8, Leg Press 4x10, Walking Lunges 3x12 (per leg), Leg Curl 3x12, Calf Raise 3x15.

Day 5 Full Body A: Barbell Bench Press 3x8, Barbell Squat 3x8, Barbell Rows 3x8, Dumbbell RDL 3x10, Push-ups 3xAMRAP, Goblet Squat 3x12.

Day 6 Full Body B: Dumbbell Bench Press 3x8, Barbell Squat 3x8, Seal Rows 3x8, Leg Press 3x10, Dumbbell Flyes 3x10, Leg Curl 3x10.

Maintain 90-minute session pacing. Rest 2-3 minutes on main compounds, 60-90 seconds on accessories.

Upper/Lower split increases frequency on your favorite lifts. Volume up 15% via added sets on rows and squats. Monitor recovery; hunger at 6/10 suggests adequate intake. Maintain current diet structure.
